mission.title: kyverno: Delete generated resource when triggering resource is deleted
mission.description: Delete generated resource when triggering resource is deleted. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current kyverno deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kyverno version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
helm list -n kyverno 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kyverno installation.

steps.title: Review kyverno configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A triggering resource and its generated resource very often share an linked life cycle where one follows the other.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Delete generated resource when triggering resource is
steps.description:
## Related issue

## Milestone of this PR
 `/milestone 1.5.0`.

## Proposed Changes
When `synchronize` is `true` - when the triggred resource is deleted the `generateRequest` get updated. On updation of the 
`generateRequests` the cleanup controller will enqueue the `generateRequests` for
```yaml
c.grInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
		AddFunc:    c.addGR,
		UpdateFunc: c.updateGR,
		DeleteFunc: c.deleteGR,
})
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Delete generated resource when triggering resource is" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Related issue

## Milestone of this PR
 `/milestone 1.5.0`.

## Proposed Changes
When `synchronize` is `true` - when the triggred resource is deleted the `generateRequest` get updated. On updation of the 
`generateRequests` the cleanup controller will enqueue the `generateRequests` for deletion.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: lbtest
spec:
  selector:
    app: lbtest
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8080
    protocol: TCP

apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: generate-prednat-gnp-for-lb-svc
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Pre DNAT Global Network Policy for LoadBalancer Service
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Network Security
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: GlobalNetworkPolicy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      By default, Ingress communication to the External IP of a LoadBalancer type
      service is denied, this policy will generate a Calico Global Network Policy
      to allow communication to the assigned LoadBalancer IP and ports specified
      in the Service resource. 
spec:
  background: true
  validationFailureAction: audit
  rules:
  - name: lb-prednat-gnp
    match:
      resources:
        kinds:
        - Service
    preconditions:
      all

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
